title: DeepSeek Launches 'Fast Mode' and 'Expert Mode' Interface — Signals V4 Multi-Tier Launch Architecture
detail: DeepSeek implemented a significant update to its chat service on April 8, introducing 'Fast Mode' and 'Expert Mode' as distinct interface tiers alongside its existing standard chat. Industry observers interpreted the update as a direct preview of the V4 product architecture: Fast Mode likely maps to a smaller, faster distilled model for low-latency consumer applications, while Expert Mode would correspond to the full-scale V4 frontier model with extended reasoning capabilities. The interface update also hinted at upcoming 'Vision Mode' and 'Agent Mode' tabs — indicating multimodal capabilities and agentic task completion in V4 that would represent a major step beyond V3's text-only architecture. The staged interface rollout is consistent with DeepSeek's established launch strategy: it previewed capabilities incrementally before the V3 launch in December 2025, allowing it to build user familiarity and test infrastructure at scale. TechNode analysts noted that the Fast/Expert tiering also suggests DeepSeek is preparing a commercial pricing model, with Expert mode likely priced at premium tiers for enterprise customers. V4 launch is expected in the final two weeks of April 2026, with estimated pricing of $0.30 per million tokens for the base model.

title: Spirit AI Robotics Raises $145M Series B at $1.4B Valuation — China AI Robotics Wave Accelerates
detail: Chinese robotics startup Spirit AI closed a ¥1 billion ($145 million) Series B round on April 8, co-led by Shunwei Capital and Yunfeng Capital, valuing the company at over ¥10 billion ($1.4 billion). Spirit AI develops AI-native humanoid and mobile manipulation robots integrating vision-language models for general-purpose industrial and commercial applications. The round extends China's leadership in AI robotics venture investment, which totaled $2.3B in 2025 — more than double the US total for the same period. The funding reflects broader Chinese tech investor conviction that robotics is the next major vertical where China's AI capabilities, domestic manufacturing scale, and cost advantages converge into global competitive advantage. Chinese AI robotics companies — including UBTECH, AgiBot, Unitree, and now Spirit AI — have proliferated in the past 18 months, attracting investment from both strategic corporate backers (Alibaba, Tencent, Meituan) and state-aligned funds. The sector is seen as a potential export category where China could achieve the same cost-dominance it achieved in EVs and solar panels, given domestic manufacturing infrastructure for the servo motors, actuators, sensors, and edge AI chips needed for humanoid robots.
